title: excoecaria reticulata
intro: Excoecaria reticulata, also known as the poison tree, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia and Australia, characterized by its distinctive reticulated leaves and toxic sap. The sap of the plant contains a toxic compound called phorbol, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Excoecaria reticulata include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's sap is often used to make topical ointments or infusions, although caution is advised due to its toxic nature and potential side effects.
